findings: Live verification confirms Summer Tanager (Piranga rubra), family Cardinalidae. The plate’s adult-male plumage, chunky large-headed profile, thick blunt pale-horn bill, dark eye, plain rounded wing, and broad medium-long tail agree with the field-reference photos and guides.
Measurements shown are correct: length 6.7–7.5 in (17–19 cm), wingspan 11–12 in (28–30 cm), and weight 0.8–1.1 oz (23–31 g).
Behavior and habitat text agrees with live references. The female/immature note is acceptable, though molting immature males may also be patchy red and yellow.
Anatomy is coherent: exactly one head, one beak, two wings, two legs with feet, and one tail. No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.

verification_sources.title: Summer Tanager Identification — All About Birds
